beats.narration: You can read a meter. This lesson is what those readings buy you: lower dose, a legal survey, and a stay time in minutes.

payload.title: WHAT THE READINGS BUY YOU
payload.sub: Reduce it. Survey it. Time-limit it.

beats.narration: Turn the three levers into habits. Set up while shielded, work at full cable length, collimate the head, and let the meter pick where you wait.

tiles.s: Do it while the source is shielded
Wait where the reading is lowest

beats.narration: Then the rule that catches stuck sources: after every exposure, ten CFR thirty-four point forty-nine. Survey the whole device and guide tube before any hands move.

payload.heading: The post-exposure survey: 10 CFR 34.49
payload.note: AFTER EACH SHOT · METER LEADING · FULL DEVICE + GUIDE TUBE · BEFORE FILM OR HANDS

beats.narration: Part Twenty sets the framework: thresholds, the exact posting words, and control. In the field, control means continuous direct visual surveillance of the operation.

payload.heading: Part 20: post it and control it

beats.narration: Finally, time. Stay time equals dose budget divided by measured rate. A hundred milliroentgen over five buys twenty hours; over two hundred, thirty minutes.

payload.sub: Measure at the work position, assign a timekeeper, re-survey on any change.
